# LUPO, expanded reference for AI assistants (llms-full)

Flattened content of lupolabs.ai's key pages. Last updated: 2026-07-13.
Canonical short map: https://lupolabs.ai/llms.txt

---

## Home (https://lupolabs.ai/)

Turn inbound noise into qualified pipeline. LUPO is the HubSpot-native AI inbound SDR with buyer intelligence built in: it sees which companies are showing intent on your site, captures every lead across web form, email, chat and phone, qualifies them, filters the noise, enriches each account and scores buying intent, and books real buyers straight into your reps' calendars, with the full reasoning written back to the CRM. Company-level and person-level identification across your traffic. Marketing generates the demand. Reps close the deals. LUPO runs everything in between.

The page hosts a live browser voice demo: "Talk to LUPO live" connects a real call to the agent, with a voice picker (several voices to audition). There is also an interactive dashboard preview showing the SDR morning view: leads by channel, intent bands, noise filtered, meetings booked.

The problem, in numbers (independent research): 28% of a sales rep's week is actually spent selling (Salesforce, State of Sales). 73% of B2B leads are not sales-ready at the moment they arrive (MarketingSherpa). 23% of inbound web leads are never responded to at all (Harvard Business Review, Oldroyd). B2B buyers spend only 17% of the buying journey with any supplier's reps (Gartner).

## Visitor identification and buyer-intelligence layer

LUPO sees which companies are showing intent on your site, with evidence attached, and resolves the leads who engage so the inbound SDR can act on them. Three layers:
- Company-level identification, across your traffic. LUPO identifies the organisations showing intent, with the evidence behind each match. Runs on all traffic.
- Person-level identification. Resolves named individuals at the identifying company across your traffic, with the evidence on the record. LUPO resolves the people who came to you, never cold strangers.
- Freemail resolution. The freemail hand-raiser that domain-based enrichment gives up on, LUPO resolves by enriching the signal they gave and, when needed, asking in the conversation. LUPO resolves the people who came to you; it does no cold identification of strangers.

Identity is an input to the inbound SDR, not the product. Once a visitor or lead is identified, LUPO qualifies, scores, routes, books, and writes back to the CRM with decision logs. Versus a horizontal visitor-identification tool (for example Warmly), the difference is what happens after the reveal: LUPO does the whole inbound SDR job, and is HubSpot-native.

## How it works (https://lupolabs.ai/how-it-works/)

The pathway from first touch to booked meeting: a visitor arrives, often before any hand-raise, and LUPO identifies the company showing intent, company-level and person-level. A lead then engages on any channel (web form, email, chat, phone), including freemail hand-raisers LUPO resolves in the conversation. LUPO answers in seconds, qualifies with real questions, enriches the lead (firmographics, technographics, decision-maker title) with live company-signal data, scores buying intent (funding, M&A, leadership hires, headcount growth), then routes: real buyers get booked into the right rep's calendar; vendors are declined and logged; support requests go to the right team with context; tire-kickers are logged for marketing. Everything syncs to the CRM and pings Slack with full context.

## For PLG and usage-based companies (https://lupolabs.ai/for/usage-based/)

The inbound SDR for usage-based, API-first, and PLG companies, where the best buyers never fill in a form. A developer signs up with Gmail, grabs an API key, and scales to production before sales ever sees a name. LUPO watches the usage, gates fraud before it scores value, scores production momentum independent of identity, opens a contextual conversation to get the invisible buyer to self-identify, and writes the account story to the CRM with an evidence string. The same inbound SDR brain, triggered by behaviour instead of a hand-raise. Best fit: CPaaS, messaging, voice, email, payments, infrastructure, dev tools, data, security, and AI platforms with meaningful freemail signup volume.

## Project River (https://lupolabs.ai/river/)

A standalone product by LUPO: the freemail identity engine. Your highest-value signups arrive on personal Gmail and Outlook with no company attached; River resolves the person and the account behind every one. Every verdict carries a calibrated confidence, the evidence chain that produced it (including the signals that voted against), and a guaranteed false-match rate at the tier that writes to the CRM. When evidence is thin, River returns unresolved and names what would settle it: unresolved stays unresolved. Not enrichment. Resolution. It works from the rows you choose to send, batch file or API call. Waitlist open on the page.

## Pricing (https://lupolabs.ai/pricing/)

Priced per pilot, depending on inbound volume and integrations. Engagements start with a 30-day founder-led pilot with agreed success criteria (qualification rate, form-to-meeting conversion, rep hours saved, leads recovered), fully live in 2 to 4 weeks.

## ROI (https://lupolabs.ai/roi/)

"The fix, in numbers": an open ROI model for the AI inbound SDR, with the methodology in visible text and an interactive calculator. Three value streams: recovered revenue (leads that currently leak; HBR's 23% never-answered baseline), research hours returned (every meeting arrives pre-briefed: contact verified, company signals, decision-maker context), and hires avoided (capacity without headcount). Net annual impact = incremental closed revenue + hiring cost avoided, minus LUPO's annual cost; rep hours are reported as capacity but not priced into the net figure, to avoid double counting; outputs are labeled revenue impact plus cost avoided, never profit. Defaults are conservative and sourced (Harvard Business Review, Salesforce State of Sales, MarketingSherpa), and every default is adjustable. Views for marketing owners (recovered leads, incremental pipeline, cost per incremental opportunity) and sales owners (rep hours returned, meetings held, hiring cost avoided).

## Channels

### Web forms (https://lupolabs.ai/channels/web-forms/)
Channel-aware response logic. A high-intent form triggers an outbound callback inside sixty seconds. A senior B2B buyer gets a personal email reply with a booking link instead: LUPO will never surprise-call a VP who submitted a demo form. Gated-content downloads count as a form channel.

### Inbound email (https://lupolabs.ai/channels/inbound-email/)
Agentic email qualification. Inbound inquiries get a personal reply with a one-click booking link. Two-way: LUPO reads replies and carries the thread to a booking or a polite close, under the same qualification policy as every other channel.

### Chat (https://lupolabs.ai/channels/chat/)
LUPO's own proactive chat agent engages visitors on landing, qualifies, and books inside the chat (it runs live on lupolabs.ai). Or the existing widget (Intercom, Drift, HubSpot Chat, or custom) captures intent and consent, then hands off to LUPO. Any widget that can post a webhook works.

### Inbound phone (https://lupolabs.ai/channels/inbound-phone/)
LUPO answers inbound calls on the second ring, 24/7. It qualifies the caller live, checks real calendar availability, and books the meeting on the call. Buying signals stated on the call feed intent scoring. Full transcripts, recordings, and outcomes are logged. The inbound channel almost nobody else covers. Anyone can hear it: the homepage hosts a live call demo.

## Integrations (https://lupolabs.ai/integrations/)

Live today: HubSpot CRM sync (https://lupolabs.ai/integrations/hubspot/), Salesforce CRM sync (https://lupolabs.ai/integrations/salesforce/), Slack alerts (https://lupolabs.ai/integrations/slack/), Google Calendar booking (https://lupolabs.ai/integrations/google-calendar/), Microsoft 365 / Outlook booking (https://lupolabs.ai/integrations/outlook/). Every qualified contact, call summary, transcript, intent score, and booking is written to the CRM automatically. Any CRM, chat, or form reachable by webhook can connect on request. Voice infrastructure: LUPO's own production-grade voice, built in-house. SMS: A2P 10DLC registered.

## Compare (https://lupolabs.ai/compare/)

- LUPO vs Qualified (https://lupolabs.ai/compare/lupo-vs-qualified/): Qualified (Piper) is the most established product in the category, built for Salesforce-based revenue teams, website-centric, custom annual enterprise pricing; Salesforce completed its acquisition of Qualified on 1 April 2026, and its future is Agentforce on the Salesforce platform; Piper is a human-styled avatar while LUPO is a transparent AI agent (peer-reviewed research links human-styled software to lower trust and purchase intent). LUPO covers HubSpot, Salesforce, or any CRM; four channels including live phone answering; every lead enriched through a triple-source data waterfall and scored with buying-intent triggers; stated pricing with a 30-day pilot. Choose Qualified if you want the AI SDR and the CRM from the same vendor, with a product roadmap Salesforce controls end to end; LUPO integrates with Salesforce through the same APIs the partner ecosystem is built on, and is the fit for HubSpot or mixed stacks, real phone volume, and enrichment plus intent scoring on every lead.
- LUPO vs Warmly (https://lupolabs.ai/compare/lupo-vs-warmly/): Warmly is a horizontal visitor-identification and engagement layer (company-level identification, website chat, sales alerts, retargeting). LUPO uses identity as an input and then does the whole inbound SDR job: qualify across web form, email, chat, and phone, resolve freemail hand-raisers in the conversation, score, route, book, and write back with decision logs, HubSpot-native. Both identify the companies and the engaged people on your site; the difference is what happens after the reveal, where LUPO runs the entire inbound SDR job. Company-level and person-level identification across your traffic.
- LUPO vs Spara (https://lupolabs.ai/compare/lupo-vs-spara/): the two closest products in the category, competing directly; both are built on the same bet, that speed wins inbound. Spara (New York, launched September 2025, 15M USD seed) runs voice, email, chat, and text AI agents plus AI-led product demos; publicly listed from 2,100 USD per month billed annually. Like Spara, LUPO engages visitors the moment they land, through its own proactive chat agent or one already running, and answers every channel in seconds: sixty-second callbacks on high-intent forms, email replies in seconds, phone on the second ring. The divide is underneath the conversation: LUPO enriches every lead with live company-signal data and scores buying intent before a rep sees it. Choose LUPO for instant engagement plus qualification depth, enrichment and buying-intent scoring on every lead, and named, live CRM integrations; Spara fits teams that want AI-led product demos or SMS as engagement surfaces.
- LUPO vs Drift (https://lupolabs.ai/compare/lupo-vs-drift/): Drift, the conversational marketing pioneer, was acquired by Salesloft in 2024 and a gradual sunset was publicly reported in March 2026. Teams migrating off Drift that used it for pipeline (qualifying visitors, booking meetings) get the same job from LUPO across chat plus phone, email, and web forms.
- LUPO vs Intercom Fin (https://lupolabs.ai/compare/lupo-vs-intercom-fin/): Fin (formerly Intercom) began in customer service and now has an inbound-sales motion (Fin for Sales: engage, qualify, book via Calendly or Chili Piper, context-rich handoff). It works with HubSpot via a connector, not natively. Salesforce signed a definitive agreement to acquire Fin for about 3.6 billion USD on 15 June 2026 (pending close, expected fiscal Q4 2027), bound for Agentforce; Fin remains standalone for now. Honest differentiators: Fin does not do automated email follow-up after qualification, and its booking is Calendly-dependent, whereas LUPO runs persistent multi-touch follow-up and native booking, is purpose-built for inbound rather than support extended into sales, and is HubSpot-native and independent. In 2026 Salesforce has moved to absorb both Qualified (inbound SDR, completed 1 April) and Fin (signed 15 June); LUPO is the independent, HubSpot-native option.
- LUPO vs Conversica (https://lupolabs.ai/compare/lupo-vs-conversica/): Conversica's Revenue Digital Assistants run persistent two-way email/SMS follow-up across large lead databases. LUPO is the real-time front door across four channels including live phone, with enrichment and intent scoring.
- LUPO vs 11x (https://lupolabs.ai/compare/lupo-vs-11x/): 11x's digital workers run outbound end to end (prospecting, outreach over email and LinkedIn). LUPO is inbound. Complementary: 11x generates cold pipeline; LUPO converts the inbound demand you already paid to create.
- Qualified alternatives for HubSpot teams (https://lupolabs.ai/compare/qualified-alternatives/): why teams look (Salesforce requirement, custom enterprise pricing, the April 2026 acquisition), and the honest six-option list (LUPO, Spara, Conversica, Intercom Fin, Drift, 11x) with LUPO's disclosure that it is our product.

## Inbound SDR guide (https://lupolabs.ai/inbound-sdr/)

The complete guide to the inbound SDR role and its automation. Definition: an inbound SDR is a sales development representative who handles the leads a company already has, responding fast, qualifying each lead, filtering noise, and booking real buyers into meetings. Covers: what the role does, where it sits (sales, marketing, or RevOps), what it costs (US ~$57k base / ~$96k total, UK ~£40k base, $110k-175k fully loaded), why it is being automated (a 2024 audit of 1,000 B2B SaaS sites found 63.5% never responded to a demo request), what an AI inbound SDR is, how it works channel by channel across web form, email, chat, and phone, how to evaluate one (channel coverage incl. real inbound phone, qualification depth, signal scoring, two-way CRM sync, audit trail, weeks-not-quarters implementation, transparent pricing), and the honest answer to "can AI replace inbound SDRs" (the triage layer yes, the sales conversation no). Supporting posts: inbound SDR vs outbound SDR (https://lupolabs.ai/blog/inbound-sdr-vs-outbound-sdr), AI inbound SDR vs chatbot (https://lupolabs.ai/blog/ai-inbound-sdr-vs-chatbot), inbound SDR metrics (https://lupolabs.ai/blog/inbound-sdr-metrics), inbound SDR cost (https://lupolabs.ai/blog/inbound-sdr-cost).

## Glossary (https://lupolabs.ai/glossary/)

- Inbound SDR (https://lupolabs.ai/glossary/inbound-sdr/): a sales development rep who handles the leads a company already has: responding fast, qualifying, filtering noise, routing, and booking real buyers into meetings. Full guide at https://lupolabs.ai/inbound-sdr/
- AI SDR (https://lupolabs.ai/glossary/ai-sdr/): an AI agent that does sales development work. The category splits in two: outbound AI SDRs that prospect cold, and AI inbound SDRs that capture, qualify, and book the leads a business already has.
- AI inbound SDR (https://lupolabs.ai/glossary/ai-inbound-sdr/): an AI agent that does the inbound half of a sales development rep's job autonomously: answering inbound across channels, qualifying, enriching, scoring buying intent, routing, and booking meetings. Distinct from outbound AI SDRs (cold prospecting) and from chatbots (single-channel, scripted).
- Inbound lead qualification (https://lupolabs.ai/glossary/inbound-lead-qualification/): deciding which inbound leads deserve a salesperson's time, separating fit (who they are) from intent (whether now is the moment), and filtering vendors, tire-kickers, support requests, and wrong-fit prospects.
- Speed to lead (https://lupolabs.ai/glossary/speed-to-lead/): the time between a lead arriving and the first meaningful response. Responding within 5 minutes makes contact 21x more likely than waiting 30 (Oldroyd, HBR), yet 23% of web leads never get a response. Speed pays most after qualification decides the lead is worth it.
- Lead routing (https://lupolabs.ai/glossary/lead-routing/): assigning qualified leads to the right rep, round-robin or rules-based by territory, product line, seniority, or intent score, with an audit trail.
- Buying-intent scoring (https://lupolabs.ai/glossary/intent-scoring/): scoring how likely a lead is to buy now, from signals like funding rounds, M&A, leadership hires, and headcount growth. Fit tells you who; intent tells you when.

## Press kit (https://lupolabs.ai/press/)

Boilerplate (50 words): LUPO is the AI inbound SDR. It captures every inbound lead across web form, email, chat, and phone, filters the noise, enriches and scores every lead for buying intent, and books real buyers straight into reps' calendars. It syncs to HubSpot, Salesforce, or any CRM.
Fast facts: Category AI inbound SDR · Channels web form, email, chat, phone · Founded 2025 · Company Flair Technologies Ltd · Pricing per pilot · Programs: ElevenLabs Grant recipient.
Contact: hello@lupolabs.ai · https://www.linkedin.com/company/luposales/

## FAQ highlights (https://lupolabs.ai/faq/)

Sales or marketing? Whoever owns inbound: sales, marketing, or RevOps. Marketing gets control and visibility over the leads it worked hard to generate; sales only sees real buyers. / Is this just a chatbot? LUPO is a qualification layer across web forms, email, chat, and phone; chat is one intake of four, via LUPO's own proactive chat agent (live on lupolabs.ai) or your existing widget handing off. / Speed? Sub-2-minute median time-to-first-action on qualified inbound, measured. / Stack? HubSpot, Salesforce, Slack, Google Calendar, Microsoft 365 / Outlook live today; webhooks for everything else.

## Blog (https://lupolabs.ai/blog/, RSS https://lupolabs.ai/blog/feed.xml)

Selected posts: "Qualified Isn't In-Market" (fit vs buying intent), "Inbound SDR Metrics: The Numbers That Predict Pipeline" (fifteen years of response-time audits in one table), "What an Inbound SDR Costs in 2026" (salary, fully loaded, AI alternative), "Inbound SDR vs Outbound SDR", "An AI Inbound SDR Is Not a Chatbot", "The Best AI Inbound SDR Tools in 2026", "The AI SDR Has Moved Inbound", "The Inbound Triage Tax", "Round Robin Is Not a Routing Strategy", "The Rep-Free Buyer Still Needs Sales", "Before You Let an AI Agent Touch Revenue, Check These 7 Things". Author: LUPO.
